name: Wyze Cam v4 Wired 2.5K Indoor/Outdoor Security Camera

specs.Storage: Free 14-day cloud events + microSD slot (32GB included)

whatWorks: 2.5K resolution at $39 CAD is extraordinary — most security cameras at this price offer 1080p, and several charge $100+ for QHD. The Wyze Cam v4's 2560×1440p image is noticeably sharper for identifying faces, license plates, and package details in playback.

A 32GB microSD card is included in the box, enabling continuous local recording with no subscription required. Most competitors at this price don't include the card — and many don't even have a microSD slot. With the included card and continuous recording enabled, you get days of 24/7 footage without spending a cent more.

5GHz Wi-Fi support is a meaningful upgrade over the v3 and most competing cameras — a 5GHz connection delivers faster live view loading and more stable streaming in environments with congested 2.4GHz networks. It's the first Wyze camera to include dual-band support.

Color night vision in well-lit areas (porch lights, street lamps) delivers color detail that infrared-only cameras miss. During daylight hours, the wide 130° field of view captures more of an entryway or backyard than most competing cameras.
worthKnowing: Color night vision requires ambient light — in true darkness, the camera falls back to infrared black-and-white. The color night vision marketing can create inflated expectations; it's better described as enhanced-light color mode rather than true low-light color capability.

The free Wyze app includes 14 days of rolling event-triggered cloud clips, which is generous. Full 24/7 cloud recording requires Wyze Cam Unlimited at $5.99/month USD. The included microSD card covers 24/7 continuous recording locally without a subscription.

Wyze has faced data security incidents in the past — a 2019 data exposure and a 2024 incident where camera feeds were briefly visible to wrong users. The company responded with fixes and security improvements, but privacy-sensitive buyers should weigh this history.

The camera is wired (USB-A to USB-C) with no battery option — it needs to be within reach of a power source or use a long USB cable run. Outdoor installation typically requires a weatherproof USB cable or an outdoor-rated power strip.

commonComplaints.title: Color night vision requires ambient light…
commonComplaints.body: Marketed as color night vision but if there's no porch light nearby it just switches to black and white IR. Don't expect it to see color in complete darkness.

commonComplaints.title: Past security incidents are a concern…
commonComplaints.body: Wyze has had some notable security issues historically. The hardware is great but I'm hesitant to put it in a bedroom for that reason. Sticking to outdoor use.

commonComplaints.title: Wired only — need power nearby…
commonComplaints.body: My ideal mounting spot for the backyard doesn't have power within reach. Had to run a 20-foot USB cable. Wish there was a battery version of the v4.

commonComplaints.title: 5GHz connection can be unstable…
commonComplaints.body: The 5GHz support is new and has had some firmware issues — I had a camera dropping to 2.4GHz constantly. Fixed in a later update but not before several weeks of frustration.

verdict: The Wyze Cam v4 is the most camera you can buy for $39 CAD — 2.5K video, 130° FOV, dual-band Wi-Fi, two-way audio, and a 32GB microSD card included. The limitations (wired power, ambient light required for color night vision, Wyze's security history) are real but shouldn't deter buyers who understand the trade-offs. For budget-first security coverage of entryways, driveways, and common areas, it's hard to beat.

realBuyers.quote: 2.5K at $39. I can read license plates on cars parked across my driveway. At this price it's borderline absurd.
The included 32GB card means I'm not paying for a subscription and I have continuous recording. That was the dealbreaker feature for me.
5GHz makes a real difference — my v3 cameras on 2.4GHz had lag and buffering. The v4 on 5GHz is smooth and fast to connect.
I have six of these covering my entire property. Total cost was less than one premium camera. Coverage per dollar is unmatched.

sources.title: Wyze Cam v4 Review — The Verge

metaDescription: Wyze Cam v4 review for Canada: 2.5K video, 32GB card included, 5GHz Wi-Fi at $39. Honest on security history, wired-only, and color night vision limits. ~$39 CAD.
introText: The Wyze Cam v4 is a wired 2.5K security camera that includes a 32GB microSD card, dual-band Wi-Fi, and 130° field of view — the most feature-packed budget security camera available. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B0C6GF9XLM) at around $39 CAD.
